definitions: a female parent
examples: My mother is a teacher.

definitions: a male parent
examples: His father works in a bank.

definitions: a person's boy child
examples: They have a young son.

definitions: a person's girl child
examples: Her daughter is five years old.

definitions: a man or boy with the same parents as another person
examples: I have one older brother.

definitions: a woman or girl with the same parents as another person
examples: She lives with her sister.

definitions: the father of a person's father or mother
examples: My grandfather is 80 years old.

definitions: the mother of a person's father or mother
examples: Her grandmother told us a story.

definitions: the son of a person's son or daughter
examples: He bought a toy for his grandson.

definitions: the daughter of a person's son or daughter
examples: Their granddaughter visits them every summer.

definitions: the brother of someone's mother or father
examples: My uncle lives in New York.

definitions: the sister of someone's father or mother
examples: Aunt Mary brought a cake.

definitions: a child of a person's aunt or uncle
examples: I am going to play with my cousin.

definitions: a son of someone's brother or sister
examples: I bought a gift for my nephew.

definitions: a daughter of someone's brother or sister
examples: My niece is very cute.

definitions: the man that a woman is married to
examples: Her husband is a doctor.

definitions: the woman that a man is married to
examples: His wife is a teacher.

definitions: a person's mother or father
examples: I live with my parents.

definitions: a young human being
examples: The child is sleeping.

definitions: each of two or more children having one or both parents in common
examples: Do you have any siblings?

definitions: a husband or wife
examples: Employees can bring their spouses to the party.

definitions: the father of one's husband or wife
examples: My father-in-law is coming for dinner.

definitions: the mother of one's husband or wife
examples: Her mother-in-law gave her a necklace.

definitions: the husband of one's daughter
examples: Our son-in-law is an engineer.

definitions: the wife of one's son
examples: She loves her daughter-in-law.

definitions: the brother of one's wife or husband
examples: My brother-in-law helped me move.

definitions: the sister of one's wife or husband
examples: Her sister-in-law is very kind.

definitions: a man who is married to one's mother after the divorce of or death of one's father
examples: He gets along well with his stepfather.

definitions: a woman who is married to one's father after the divorce of or death of one's mother
examples: Cinderella had a cruel stepmother.

definitions: a child whose parents are dead
examples: He was an orphan raised by his aunt.

definitions: a woman who has lost her spouse by death
examples: The widow wore black to the funeral.

definitions: a man who has lost his spouse by death
examples: The widower lived alone in the big house.

definitions: a person whom one knows and with whom one has a bond of mutual affection
examples: She is my best friend.

definitions: a person who is actively opposed or hostile to someone or something
examples: The soldier faced his enemy.

definitions: either of a pair of people engaged together in the same activity
examples: He is my business partner.

definitions: a person with whom one works in a profession or business
examples: I had lunch with a colleague.

definitions: a person one knows slightly, but who is not a close friend
examples: He is an acquaintance from work.

definitions: a person in charge of a worker or organization
examples: My boss gave me a promotion.

definitions: a person employed for wages or salary
examples: The company has 500 employees.

definitions: a person or organization that employs people
examples: Her employer provides health insurance.

definitions: a person living near or next door to the speaker
examples: My neighbor has a friendly dog.

definitions: a person occupying the same room as another
examples: My college roommate was very neat.

definitions: a member of the same class in a school or college
examples: I met an old classmate at the mall.

definitions: a fellow member of a team
examples: He passed the ball to his teammate.

definitions: an experienced and trusted adviser
examples: She was my mentor in the early days of my career.

definitions: a regular male companion with whom a person has a romantic relationship
examples: She went to the movies with her boyfriend.

definitions: a regular female companion with whom a person has a romantic relationship
examples: He bought flowers for his girlfriend.

definitions: a man to whom someone is engaged to be married
examples: Her fianc√© bought her a diamond ring.

definitions: a woman to whom someone is engaged to be married
examples: His fianc√©e is a nurse.

definitions: a woman on her wedding day
examples: The bride looked beautiful in her dress.

definitions: a man on his wedding day
examples: The groom waited at the altar.

definitions: a person connected by blood or marriage
examples: Many relatives came to the party.

definitions: a person, typically one more remote than a grandparent, from whom one is descended
examples: My ancestors came from Italy.

definitions: a person that is descended from a particular ancestor
examples: She is a descendant of a royal family.

definitions: two people who are married or in a romantic relationship
examples: They are a lovely couple.

definitions: a person with whom one spends a lot of time
examples: His dog is his constant companion.

definitions: a human being regarded as an individual
examples: She is a very nice person.

definitions: a human being
examples: We are all humans.

definitions: an adult male human being
examples: That man is my teacher.

definitions: an adult female human being
examples: The woman in the red dress is my aunt.

definitions: a male child or young man
examples: The boy is playing with a ball.

definitions: a female child or young woman
examples: The girl is reading a book.

definitions: a very young child
examples: The baby is crying.

definitions: a very young child or baby
examples: Infants need a lot of sleep.

definitions: a young child who is just beginning to walk
examples: The toddler fell down while playing.

definitions: a person aged between 13 and 19 years
examples: Many teenagers like listening to pop music.

definitions: a person who is fully grown or developed
examples: Adults have to pay full price for tickets.

definitions: an elderly person
examples: The local center offers activities for seniors.

definitions: young people considered as a group
examples: The youth of today are very tech-savvy.

definitions: a large number of people gathered together
examples: A big crowd watched the concert.

definitions: the assembled spectators or listeners at a public event
examples: The audience clapped loudly.

definitions: a single human being as distinct from a group
examples: Every individual has rights.

definitions: a legally recognized subject or national of a state or commonwealth
examples: She is a citizen of India.

definitions: a male person or animal
examples: The survey was taken by both males and females.

definitions: a female person or animal
examples: There are more females than males in the class.

definitions: human beings considered collectively
examples: This discovery is a giant leap for mankind.

definitions: the human race; human beings collectively
examples: Crimes against humanity will not be tolerated.

definitions: the aggregate of people living together in a more or less ordered community
examples: We must work together to build a better society.

definitions: all the inhabitants of a particular town, area, or country
examples: The population of the city is growing rapidly.

definitions: all of the people born and living at about the same time
examples: The younger generation uses technology more.

definitions: a person who is invited to visit someone's home
examples: We have guests staying for the weekend.

definitions: a person who receives or entertains other people as guests
examples: The host welcomed us at the door.
